data.description: Monro (NASDAQ: MNRO) is set to release its quarterly earnings on January 28, 2026. Analysts emphasize the importance of understanding market sentiments and industry expectations in decision-making, thus it's crucial for investors to stay informed about Monro's performance in the industry.

data.title: TI reported Q4 2025 revenue of $4.42 billion, net income of $1.16 billion. They invested $3.9B in R&D, $4.6B in capex, $6.5B returned to owners. Free cash flow: $2.9B. Next quarter outlook: revenue $4.32B-$4.68B, EPS $1.22-$1.48. Dividends paid: $4,999M, stock repurchases: $1,477M. Total cash returned to shareholders: $6,476M.
data.description: Texas Instruments (TI) reported its fourth quarter revenue at $4.42 billion, with net income hitting $1.16 billion, leading to earnings per share of $1.27. Despite a 6-cent reduction in EPS that was unexpected, TI's revenue decreased by 7% sequentially but increased by 10% from the same quarter the previous year. The company's cash flow from operations for the trailing 12 months stood at $7.2 billion, highlighting the strength of their business model. TI invested heavily in R&D and SG&A, capital expenditures, and returned $6.5 billion to its owners over the past year. Looking ahead, TI's first-quarter outlook predicts revenue in the range of $4.32 billion to $4.68 billion and earnings per share between $1.22 and $1.48. The cash flow from operations and free cash flow for the same period showed significant increases. Additionally, the company paid dividends of $1.29 billion and repurchased $403 million in stock. In summary, despite challenges, TI's financial results demonstrate stability and growth in various sectors, leading to positive returns for shareholders.

data.title: Riverview Bancorp reports $1.4M net income in Q3 2026 - $10.5M net interest income, 2.96% interest margin, 0.07% NPAs. Tangible book value per share at $6.62. Branches in Vancouver and over $1.5B in total assets. Shareholders return $0.02 dividend per share. Company's strategic focus on asset returns, new revenue streams, and optimized operations. Increased focus on commercial and business banking, digital innovation, and customer experience. Strong loan growth and robust financial metrics.
data.description: Riverview Bancorp, Inc. reported earnings of $1.4 million in their third fiscal quarter ending December 31, 2025, with a diluted earnings per common share of $0.07. They also highlighted a tangible book value per share of $6.62. Net interest income for the quarter was $10.5 million, with a net interest margin of 2.96%. Non-performing assets were at 0.07% of total assets. The company recorded non-interest income of $3.5 million and non-interest expense of $12.2 million. They paid a cash dividend of $0.02 per share and completed a $2.0 million stock repurchase plan. Total loans increased to $1.07 billion, while total deposits decreased to $1.23 billion. The company maintained strong capital levels, with a total risk-based capital ratio of 16.35% and a Tier 1 leverage ratio of 11.24%. Tangible book value per share increased to $6.62. Non-performing loans were at 0.10% of total loans, while net charge-offs were $246,000. The company has approximately $515.5 million in available liquidity and maintains an efficient operating profile. Their strategic focus includes expanding commercial loans, growing business banking, and enhancing treasury management platforms. Riverview continues to serve its community with a customer-centric approach and is positioned well for sustainable growth and digital innovation.

data.title: February could be Bitcoin's 'Uptober' with strong historical performance data backing it as a promising month for BTC.
data.description: February has historically been a strong performing month for Bitcoin (BTC), leading Bitcoin network economist Timothy Peterson to refer to it as the "real Uptober." Data indicates that this month tends to see positive growth for BTC. Will this trend continue in the upcoming month?

data.title: FIA plans for WRC return to US with candidate event set for mid-2026, organized with ACCUS from 11-17 June.
data.description: The Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA) is intensifying efforts for the FIA World Rally Championship (WRC) comeback in the United States, with plans for a candidate event set for mid-2026. In partnership with the Automobile Competition Committee of the United States (ACCUS), the event is scheduled from June 11-17. The FIA aims to bring WRC back to the US, working towards global expansion and increased participation in the championship. Such initiatives demonstrate FIA's commitment to enhancing the presence of motor sport in the US, with the candidate event serving as a crucial step in this strategic plan.

data.title: Mobileum introduces GlobalRoamer® for FIFA World Cup 2026, ensuring smooth 5G voice performance in North America.
data.description: In Cupertino, California, Mobileum has introduced the GlobalRoamer® package for the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026. This offering aims to guarantee smooth roaming and optimal 5G voice quality throughout North America during the event.
